$comment: Source of truth for AutoFactory Phase 1 routing tags (issue #9 item #5). Each agent emits tags via tag_conversation (or a write tool sets them); the graph edges and the approval gate route on them. This registry is validated by `npm run check:configs` against the graph (config/agentcontrol/graphs/), the agent instructions (config/agentcontrol/ai-configs/), and the README table — so a renamed/removed/unconsumed tag fails the build instead of silently stalling the chain. Agents may emit additional INFORMATIONAL tags not listed here (e.g. tests_generated); only tags the system routes/decides on belong in this registry.

skip_flagging.description: true when the PR needs no flag (Rule F11: infra/docs/chore). Short-circuits the chain at research; approval treats it as a successful no-op, not a rejection.

flag_worthy.description: The research planner's flag-worthiness recommendation. Advisory: no edge or approval logic consumes it (skip_flagging is the actual routing signal). Forced by NODE_REQUIRED_TAGS so the planner always records a recommendation.

flag_created.description: true when a flag was created. Set automatically by the create_flag tool. Gates the hand-off to the metrics author.

flag_key.description: The created flag's key. Set automatically by create_flag. Used for the release manifest and the editor's LaunchDarkly links.

needs_tests.description: true to route to the testing agent. Forced by NODE_REQUIRED_TAGS so the metrics author always hands off.

metrics_created.description: true if any guarded-release metric was created or reused. Set automatically by the create_metric tool.

metric_keys.description: Comma-separated metric keys attached. Set automatically by create_metric. Written into the release manifest and the editor's links.

review_approved.description: The reviewer's verdict (approve/approved/true vs reject/rejected/false). Read by the approval gate. Forced by NODE_REQUIRED_TAGS so the reviewer always records a verdict; absence means the reviewer never ran (INCOMPLETE), not a rejection.

risk_level.description: low / medium / high. Read by the approval gate; gates the `middle` mode (high risk requires a human).
